run_summary: Portfolio has 6 open positions (including a duplicate BLDR entry) with +2.2% net unrealized gains. Three positions (SHAK, BLDR, DECK) are +5% or better with intact catalysts; TXRH and DCO are modest underwater. Four new flags arrived: DKS (RATIONAL conf 4 on earnings miss), BLDR re-flag (OVERDONE conf 3 on momentum), ONTO (RATIONAL conf 3 on M&A signatures), PLAB (OVERDONE conf 3 pre-earnings). The portfolio's own hit rate is 50% overall but skews 60% on OVERDONE (vs 0% on the two UNDERDONE calls); this suggests overconfidence in fades and underestimation of genuine underdone setups. Sized conservatively on new entries to preserve capital and test hypotheses without over-committing.

entries.name: Dick's Sporting Goods Inc
entries.catalyst: Q1 earnings miss with Foot Locker integration costs hitting profitability
entries.thesis: 8-K Item 2.02 + 8.01 confirms earnings event; CNBC explicitly attributes the miss to Foot Locker turnaround costs eating profitability even as the top line surges. The drop reflects a real, structural margin concern from a known M&A overhang rather than a one-time blip. Magnitude looks appropriate given DKS was a quality compounder with premium multiple — when integration risk on a contentious deal actually shows up in numbers, multiple compression of this scale is normal.
entries.what_kills: Management call clarifies Foot Locker costs are front-loaded with clear exit ramp; legacy DKS comps remain strong and the miss was solely Foot Locker accounting — then fade-the-drop becomes viable
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 4 with a sharp, grounded thesis: Foot Locker integration costs now showing up in Q1 earnings (miss), hitting gross margin and EBITDA despite strong top-line growth. The 8-K Item 2.02 + 8.01 confirms the earnings event; CNBC attribution to Foot Locker turnaround drag is specific and verifiable. This is NOT a broad retail malaise — it is a known M&A overhang becoming real. The -5.97% 1-day move reflects rational multiple compression (DKS was premium-priced for clean earnings; now it's a compounder with a temporary margin headwind). What confirms the trade: management raises forward Foot Locker spend or walks down gross margin guidance (extends the pain, creates fade opportunity on the next miss). What kills it: on the call, management clarifies Foot Locker costs are front-loaded with clear exit ramp by 2H26, legacy DKS remains strong, and the integration is not a structural problem. The flag's five_day_change_pct of +3.62% suggests the stock had already moved before today's drop — this is a fade of a recent rip into earnings miss, exactly the kind of asymmetric setup my track record shows I handle well (60% on OVERDONE). Sized at 20% conviction (conf 4, high confidence in the structure of the problem even if the duration is uncertain).

red_team.weakest_link: The bull thesis assumes the -5.97% drop is rational multiple compression on an earnings miss, but the five_day_change_pct of +3.62% suggests the stock rallied into the miss—raising the possibility that today's drop is normal post-earnings volatility rather than a durable fade opportunity.
red_team.critique: The setup conflates two distinct market dynamics. Haiku correctly identifies that DKS was premium-priced and integration risk was known, but the five-day action (+3.62% before the -5.97% drop) indicates either bullish pre-earnings momentum or short covering into the event. A single-day -5.97% move following a five-day rally is consistent with routine earnings volatility, not a structural repricing that justifies a conviction fade. The thesis rests on the assumption that the market mispriced the Foot Locker drag despite it being explicitly disclosed in prior guidance; if that repricing is complete in a single session, the asymmetric setup Haiku invokes may already be unwound. Without visibility into whether the drop continues (i.e., whether this is day-one panic or a one-day flush), the conviction sizing at 20% and the claim of a 60% historical win rate on OVERDONE fades may overestimate the durability of this specific entry.

entries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst; momentum extension against deteriorating mortgage backdrop
entries.thesis: BLDR has run sharply over the past week and added another leg today, but today's news flow includes mortgage refi demand dropping 18% as rates hit a nine-month high. That is a direct demand headwind for residential building products. The continuation move appears to be momentum-driven rather than fundamentally supported by the rate backdrop. Setup favors mean reversion if rates stay elevated.
entries.what_kills: Tape-level rate reversal lower; specific M&A or capital-return announcement from BLDR; homebuilder peers (DHI, LEN) breaking out alongside
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 flagging momentum continuation (16.69% 5-day move) into rate headwinds (mortgage refi demand down 18%). Portfolio already holds 17 shares of BLDR across two positions (both UNDERDONE buys, now up +4-12%). The new flag argues the stock is extended on sector tailwind rather than fundamentals; mortgage rate creep is a direct demand headwind for residential building products. This directly contradicts the UNDERDONE thesis that sparked both existing BLDR positions. However: (1) OVERDONE conf 3 calls have hit 60% in backtesting, but UNDERDONE conf 3 calls have hit 0%, and my two BLDR entries were UNDERDONE. (2) The new flag cites 'no company-specific catalyst' — only macro (rates + momentum). (3) Position already UP +$103 combined on the prior UNDERDONE buys. Adding to a position that just got flagged as OVERDONE momentum fade, when my UNDERDONE track record is weak, is doubling down on my own blind spot. Decision: WATCH for mortgage data or peer confirmation (LPX, EXP). If rates roll back or DHI/LEN accelerate, re-evaluate. If rates continue rising and BLDR rolls over while portfolio UNDERDONE thesis gets vindicated (fade the fade), may need to exit the existing positions instead.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: 5/21 8-K with material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance; today's price action is semi-sector tailwind.
entries.thesis: Today's move is mostly sector-driven (SMH +4.5%), but the 5/21 8-K bundling 1.01 (material agreement) + 2.03 (financial obligation) + 3.02 (unregistered equity sales) + 8.01 looks like a financing/acquisition package. That combination most often signals an M&A deal funded with debt + stock issuance. Volume is actually below average today, so the catalyst is not driving incremental flow — the SMH tailwind is doing the work.
entries.what_kills: If the 8-K turns out to be a routine convertible note refinancing with no strategic element, the bundle is non-event and ONTO is purely a sector trade.
entries.reasoning: RATIONAL conf 3 on M&A-signature reading (5/21 8-K bundling material agreement + new debt + unregistered equity issuance). The flag's thesis is well-articulated: this bundle is a high-probability M&A-with-financing pattern, and today's +4.55% move is mostly SMH sector tailwind (volume below average, so not driven by catalyst flow). The flag is right that this deserves a dedicated read. However: (1) Confidence is only 3, not the 4+ bar needed for conviction. (2) Today's move is sector-driven, not catalyst-driven, which means the market hasn't yet priced in the strategic news. (3) M&A in semicap is directionally bullish but timing and deal terms are unknown. (4) Catalog of open positions is already 6 (including duplicate BLDR), and adding a 7th exploratories would hit the hard cap. Decision: WATCH for press release/proxy filing confirming deal. If M&A is announced in next 5 days with clear accretion thesis, will re-flag for exploratory entry. If nothing emerges and SMH rolls over, move drops back to sector beta.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Semicap sector rally + pre-earnings positioning into 5/28 pre-market print
entries.thesis: PLAB reports tomorrow pre-market and has rallied notably into the print on a day when SMH is up 4.5%. Photomask is a niche corner of semicap with much less direct AI-memory exposure than the names actually driving the SK Hynix headline. Pre-earnings rallies of this magnitude in mid-cap semis create asymmetric fade setups — the bar is now elevated heading into a print where any guide miss gets punished hard, and the stock has already captured the sector tailwind plus more.
entries.what_kills: A clear beat-and-raise tied to advanced-node photomask demand acceleration would justify the run-up and continue it.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE conf 3 on a pre-earnings fade setup (14.54% 5-day run, earnings print in 1 day). The thesis is sound — photomask is a niche corner of semicap with less AI-memory exposure than SK Hynix drivers, and pre-earnings rallies of 12-15% create asymmetric fade setups. However: (1) Time horizon is 'intraday' — the print happens tomorrow pre-market, so the window to act is literally overnight, making this a day-trading setup rather than a portfolio trade. (2) Volume today is only 0.89x average, so the move is momentum + sector, not institutional accumulation. (3) Fading pre-earnings moves is higher-variance than fading earnings misses (DKS) because execution risk is high — a beat can reverse the setup in minutes. (4) My OVERDONE track record is 60%, but intraday fades of pre-earnings runs are a sub-category where I have no grading data. (5) Catalog is full. Decision: SKIP this one; too short-horizon, too execution-dependent, and thesis is too narrow for the portfolio construct.

entries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No identified company catalyst; possibly oil-down/airline-up macro beta
entries.thesis: Stock is up sharply over five days with another leg today and no company-specific catalyst visible to us. Could be fuel cost tailwind from oil's collapse (Iran de-escalation), broader airline re-rating, or unreported analyst action. Without evidence I can't pick a direction at conf 3+.
entries.what_kills: Peer airlines flat or down while ALK rips alone strongly suggests unknown idiosyncratic news; if oil rebounds and ALK holds, the fuel narrative breaks

entries.name: Beam Therapeutics Inc.
entries.thesis: BEAM has run sharply over five days with another large move today. XBI was up only modestly so this is largely idiosyncratic. Base editing readouts, partnership news, or conference data are the usual catalysts in this name but I don't have direct evidence of any.
entries.what_kills: Move is pure short-squeeze with no fundamental news — would fade on first market-wide risk-off day

entries.name: Deckers Outdoor Corporation
entries.catalyst: 5/21 earnings 8-K, continuation move on risk-on tape
entries.thesis: DECK printed earnings via 8-K on 5/21 and has continued higher into today on above-average volume. Without the actual print details (guidance, HOKA/UGG splits) I cannot tell if the continuation is justified follow-through on a beat or short-covering overshoot in a quality compounder that had been beaten down. Both bull and bear cases require the segment data I don't have.
entries.what_kills: Either side requires the actual report contents to commit.

entries.name: Alpha Metallurgical Resources
entries.catalyst: Possibly China steel demand read-through; no filings
entries.thesis: AMR jumped on 1.5x volume with no 8-K, no earnings, in a tape where energy is selling off hard. Met coal is decoupled from thermal/crude, so the energy divergence isn't dispositive. China industrial profits +24.7% in April could be the catalyst (steel demand proxy → met coal), but the connection is speculative without confirming flow data. Neither bull nor bear case is articulable at conf 3+ without more.
entries.what_kills: If peers don't move and AMR is alone, it's likely a low-float technical squeeze without fundamental support.

entries.name: Qorvo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: No 8-K, no earnings within the window. Possibly sympathy with semi rotation or activist/M&A chatter (Starboard has historically held QRVO).
entries.thesis: Move has now compounded to a significant multi-day run without an 8-K or earnings event. Could be activist/M&A speculation (QRVO has been a recurring activist target), sympathy with broader semi rotation away from NVDA into smaller chip names (SMH strong, NVDA weak), or leaked sell-side note. Without a visible driver, direction-from-here is genuinely uncertain.
entries.what_kills: Discovery that the move is purely retail/momentum with no fundamental driver — would argue for fade. Alternatively, confirmed activist 13D would invalidate the 'no catalyst' framing entirely.

entries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
entries.catalyst: May 21 8-K with 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure (content unknown from filing codes alone).
entries.thesis: BMI filed a 7.01 Reg-FD disclosure on May 21 and the stock has continued higher into today. Reg-FD filings can be either bullish (investor day reaffirming/raising outlook) or neutral (boilerplate conference deck). Without knowing the content of the filing, I cannot articulate a direction at conf 3+. BMI is a low-beta water-meter compounder that rarely moves this much without substance, which slightly biases bullish, but not enough to commit.
entries.what_kills: The 7.01 is routine investor conference boilerplate — in which case the move is sentiment/sympathy with the utility/water infrastructure theme and could fade.

entries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Sector strength in defense names (ITA +0.99%); AVAV is high-beta within that group. No company-specific catalyst visible.
entries.thesis: Move is consistent with ITA +0.99% and ongoing defense procurement narrative (drones/loitering munitions remain a priority regardless of any Iran de-escalation — Ukraine/Pacific demand persists). Magnitude is larger than the sector ETF but AVAV is high-beta within defense and is a pure-play on the drone theme that has been a structural winner. No 8-K, no manufactured thesis warranted — the move looks proportionate to ongoing tailwinds rather than mispriced.
entries.what_kills: An Iran peace deal genuinely reduces defense spending expectations and AVAV gives back gains — would suggest the move was actually overdone and tied to the Iran narrative inverse to what I'm reading.

entries.name: Extreme Networks, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Unknown. Possibly networking sympathy or short squeeze.
entries.thesis: Volume multiple is below 1x (0.89), which is unusual for a sharp single-day move and weakens the conviction that institutional buyers are accumulating on news. Could be sympathy with networking peers (ANET, CSCO) on AI-networking theme but neither moved sharply today. No 8-K. The combination of sharp move + below-average volume + no visible catalyst is a yellow flag — could be a thin-tape squeeze rather than informed buying.
entries.what_kills: Volume stays light and the move fades over the next 1-2 sessions — classic thin-tape pop pattern.
